sentences.text: Hey, Matthew!
Hey, how are you doing?
Doing well, thank you.
How are you?
Good, how was your long weekend?
Oh, relaxing for once, which was very well needed.
It does.
What's yours?
Much of the same.
We, did, like, Topgolf and cleaned the garage yesterday, so…
That sounds fun.
Yeah.
active.
It was productive.
It was like… I don't know how it's been for you, but work's been so busy lately, I haven't had, like, the personal life time to do that sort of thing, so it was nice to have that extra day.
I do get that.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Yeah.
Oh, nice!
Well, how are you settling in there?
Settling well, yeah, uncovering a lot, so it's going well, but definitely, I think, still kind of drinking from the fire hose here.
Gotcha.

I just wanted to check in, see how things are being received internally, see if you have any questions or clarifications I can make, and then, obviously the elf into the room is what we can do in terms of, like, maximum at the properties, and see if we can help you out there.
Right.
Yeah, I appreciate it.

So, full transparency, similar to, like, what I said before, I haven't been able to really share it so much globally just yet, just because I do want to make sure that we're kind of aligned with where we can get with the pricing structure.
Okay.
I fully am aware that we're not going to be able to get to where we were previously when I was at Gates, so I totally understand that.
But when it comes to the per unit cost and also a cap.
That's also where I kind of have a little bit of pause with it, just because, especially for a lot of our communities that are affordable.
we would rather spend the money that we have in advertising for actual advertising as opposed to a research tool, no matter how helpful it's going to be.
So, that's kind of my main pause.
A lot of our communities are between, like, the maybe, like, 150, and then upwards, the larger ones are, like, the 500s.
Okay.

So, really just looking to see how we can possibly max that at a certain point, or if that's not possible, maybe seeing what other solutions we might have, whether that's a per-user basis or anything like that.
But, really, that's kind of my main… stopgap right now.
Okay, and what was it?
What's the total unit count again?
We have about 5,000.
About $5,000.
Then what percentage of that is affordable versus conventional?
So we have a lot of mixed as well.
I'd probably venture to say at least half have a… have an aspect of affordable, or at least mixed.
Okay.

And then… maybe this is, like, okay, so, do you know the breakdown, I guess, of, like, what percent is, like, pure affordable, what percent is mixed, and then what percent is, like, purely conventional?
I don't have that yet, but I could probably find it.
Okay.
And where do we… what was pricing that we discussed?
Probably 85 cents a unit, I'm guessing?
Yes.
Yeah, okay.
So, and is the intention to have this… I think we talked about this, the intention would be to have this at every site with access to on-site all the way up to leadership?
Is that…
I'd love to have it on every site, just because I think it's useful.
If not at the bare minimum, I think probably at starting at market rate and then moving forward.
But I see the benefit for everyone, so…
Okay Because, like, the solution that I'm thinking of is just doing, like, a… so the next bracket, $6,000 to $12,000, is 60 cents a unit.
We can move you there, I mean, if you're looking at this, say you're going 5,000 times… 85, even if you're putting that… the minimum is about 1,000 of the units, so you go…
Yeah, it's like $42.
50 with some max cap on that.
And then, where do we put the cap at?
Okay.
Yeah, because the user… the user route is… affordable up until 10 users?
More affordable up until 10 users, and then it's essentially, like, more?
So, let's see…
And now, if we were to do the user basis, would I still be able to do scheduled reports for each of the properties, too?
So, if we were to just, like, have the corporate main office employees have access and then schedule it out to the communities?
Yeah, we just put in a blocker on that, actually.
Okay.
There is a, like, much smaller competitor of us that was, doing a similar thing to that.
They put in a report restriction, we did the same thing.
Let's see…
Where did we put that at?
Typically, we cap at 300 units.
Does that sound right?
Oh, 400, okay.
So we can get that moved down, so typically we can cap at 300 units.
And then, let me just work with finance to see if we can go 60 cent a unit.
Okay.
You've got… what are those two large properties?
There's your full property list there, I think, right?
Edith, did I send it to you already?
You did, yep.
Oh, great, okay.
Yeah, so let me just, like, I can just run, like, an actual breakdown of that and send it back to you, so you have, like, the full cost quote, essentially.
Okay, yeah, that'd be amazing, thank you.
Awesome.
And you're thinking about this just operationally, right?
And there's components of… that we walk through that are more research-based or for underwriting, but for now, you're just thinking about this from an operational perspective?
Right, I'd like to branch out if possible, if it's received well throughout, but for right now, I think the main benefit would be for the site teams.
Okay, awesome.

Probably what you will do, just to get that buy-in, if we are able to move forward, is get you, like, 2 or 3 seats of that research and that explore function, because, essentially, like, effectively what that is, is it'll give you some ammo to pitch it internally, I think, as well.
Functionally, that will be a competitor to CoStar.
Okay.
Yeah, that's my thoughts.
Supply information, demographic information, transactions,

anything you'd want to know about, like, how a market is performing, and then how an asset is, like, performing within that market, and how it's trending over time, you're able to do so within that tool.
So if you're doing a lot of research, it's like, where's the next acquisition or development?
Some benefit there.
Awesome.
So even if it's just, like, yourself and the president or something like that, just to sweeten the pot a little bit.
Perfect.
Cool.
Let me run those numbers, I'll fire them back to you, and then, it's a Tuesday, Monday, so I'll give you some time back in your day, and, let me know if I can help with anything from here.
Sounds great, thanks so much.
Alright, sounds good.
We'll see ya.
Bye-bye.
Bye.
